2606.06474.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
2606.06474.paper_summary: Discrete diffusion language models generate text by iteratively denoising an entire response in parallel. At each step, they predict tentative tokens for every masked position, committing the confident predictions to the output and discarding the unconfident ones. We show that the discarded tokens are in fact a useful lookahead signal for retrieval-augmented generation: even low-confidence tokens often surface salient entities early in the denoising trajectory, enabling retrieval of stronger evidence before the output is finalized. We exploit this through Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models (SARDI), a dynamic RAG framework that uses these lookahead tokens to guide retrieval during denoising. SARDI is training-free, retriever-agnostic, and applicable to any reasoning-capable discrete diffusion language model. Across five multi-hop QA benchmarks, SARDI outperforms current training-free diffusion and autoregressive retrieval baselines at up to $8\times$ higher throughput.
2606.06474.paper_summary_zh: 離散擴散語言模型透過平行地迭代去噪整個回應來生成文本。<lb/>在每一步中，它們為每個被遮蔽的位置預測暫定的標記，將自信的預測提交到輸出中，並丟棄不自信的預測。<lb/>我們展示了被丟棄的標記實際上是一個有用的前瞻信號，用於檢索增強生成：即使是低自信的標記，通常也會在去噪過程中早期顯現出顯著的實體，使得在輸出最終確定之前能夠檢索到更強的證據。<lb/>我們通過自增強檢索擴散語言模型（SARDI）來利用這一點，這是一個動態的RAG框架，利用這些前瞻標記來指導去噪過程中的檢索。<lb/>SARDI是無需訓練的，與檢索器無關，並且適用於任何具備推理能力的離散擴散語言模型。<lb/>在五個多跳問答基準測試中，SARDI在高達$8\times$的吞吐量下超越了當前無需訓練的擴散和自回歸檢索基準。
2606.06474.author: Paul Jünger et.al.
2606.06474.authors: Paul Jünger, Justin Lovelace, Linxi Zhao, Dongyoung Go, Kilian Q. Weinberger
